rental_deposit.title: H S Bedi vs National Highway Authority Of India (Delhi High Court, 2015)
rental_deposit.text: In this case, a tenant argued they could keep possession of a rented property until their security deposit was refunded. The Delhi High Court held that a tenant cannot withhold possession on this basis alone - the tenant has a separate legal remedy to recover the security deposit, and withholding possession instead makes the tenant liable to keep paying rent until possession is actually handed back.

rental_deposit.title: Tamil Nadu Handloom Weavers' Society vs Harbans Lal Gupta (2009)
rental_deposit.text: This case involved a tenant who terminated a lease with the notice period specified in the lease deed and then sought the return of the remaining security deposit balance from the landlord, illustrating a standard deposit-recovery dispute following lawful lease termination.
